response: {"purpose":"Figures 1b and 1c examine the read processing efficiency and PCR duplicate removal performance of the eCLIP-seq protocol compared to iCLIP. Panel 1b illustrates how the introduction of random-mer sequences during library preparation allows PCR duplicate reads—those mapping to the same genomic position with identical random-mers—to be identified and discarded, thereby quantifying the proportion of \"usable reads\" retained after each processing step. Panel 1c demonstrates, through downsampling experiments on RBFOX2 iCLIP and eCLIP datasets, how the fraction of unique, non-duplicate reads scales with sequencing depth, highlighting eCLIP's improved efficiency in recovering informative reads. Together, these panels establish that the eCLIP methodology yields a substantially higher proportion of usable reads relative to iCLIP, which is critical for robust and reproducible identification of RBP binding sites.","title":"eCLIP reduces PCR duplicates and improves usable read yield"}
